next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 17

By the third week of March, Berlint had started thinking about other things. Anya noticed it on the tram to WISE. The air still wanted a coat and gloves. The thoughts didn't. All winter the city had been thinking about arrests, investigations, borders, who would take the fall and who would get paid to bury the embarrassing parts. Now the noise had thinned into ordinary stuff. Paint samples. Grocery lists. A man worrying about his mother's hip surgery. A teenager wondering if his jacket made him look stupid. The Renewal Front had gone where scandals went after the shouting. Courtrooms. Committees. Procedural language. Both governments were doing accountability in public and burial work in private. Damian still showed up in the papers, but in the tired middle now. Consultations. Threats. Reform panels. He was not a hero and not a disgrace. Just inconveniently real.

Inside WISE, the restructuring showed. Falk's desk in the analyst pool had been cleared within days of his arrest, but the gap still held its shape. New faces. New security checks. Sylvia's review board had gone through the place hard enough that even the coffee tasted more cautious.

Sylvia had a file open on her desk when Anya came in. She closed it before Anya could read the label. The thought on the surface was quick and sour: *record. gaps. should've seen it sooner.*

"Sit down."

Anya sat.

"The review board has completed its assessment of your record. Your field status is reinstated, effective immediately. The cover identity remains intact. The summit exposure stayed in political channels, not...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya tells Damian she is a telepath — not strategically, but because she cannot build something real on asymmetry
Damian's reaction arc: anger, horror at the scope, then the complicated thing underneath
Anya does not defend herself — she sits with earned anger
Damian's counter-revelation: he has known she is Anya Forger since the gala — he confirmed it, chose to continue the alliance, met her family knowing everything except the telepathy
The asymmetry inverts and collapses: she read his mind without consent, he knew her identity without disclosure — they both operated inside knowledge the other didn't know they had
The core vulnerability: Anya does not know which of her feelings for him are hers and which are absorbed from his
He needs time. Before he leaves, he says one thing knowing she will hear the thought behind it. The thought matches.

payoffs_to_land.description: The moral asymmetry seeded in Ch 3 and crystallized in Ch 9 is finally confronted — but doubled by Damian's counter-revelation that he also held hidden knowledge
Damian's recognition, which built from Ch 2 through Ch 12 and was confirmed in Ch 13, is disclosed to Anya — completing the thread
The Eden Academy photo from Ch 7 resonates — Damian kept it, used it to confirm her identity, and now both know

prev_tail_excerpt:
...ent could wear the exact clothes of a good one.

Anya opened her mouth. She wanted to say all of it at once, Damian, the first lie, the reason for it, the way every choice after that had gotten harder and smaller and worse.

"Not here," Loid said.

Quiet. Precise. Final.

She nodded.

Outside, the summit was breaking open. Press were already at the barricades. A cameraman near the front steps was thinking *Desmond's finished* while the producer beside him thought *No, Desmond just saved the minister,* and kept saying Damian's surname into a handset like he was trying out headlines. On a security radio, somebody asked for camera six again, the one with the woman on the south doors, and got told to wait for clearance.

Anya kept walking until the cold hit her. She put one hand on the stone wall and bent her head. The relay's green indicator stayed on the back of her eyes even with them shut. Eleven minutes and forty seconds. Long enough to turn one missed fragment into something that would keep moving after today.

Loid found her there a few minutes later. He came out with his belt missing and her folded note in one hand. He stopped beside her and looked through the glass with her.

"You did good work today."

"Dad..."

"Later."

He folded the note once more and put it in his pocket. His shoulder touched hers through both jackets and stayed there.

Sirens climbed the street. The conference center kept spilling people into the cold. By then Falk was in custody, and Damian's name was already out in the street wearing arguments he hadn't chosen. Neither of them went back inside.
state_deltas_required: the moral weight of Anya's lifelong secret AND Damian's counter-knowledge — both have been deceiving each other

Both secrets are out. Anya discloses her telepathy; Damian reveals he has known her identity since the gala. The relationship is remade on genuinely mutual terms — both deceived, both chose each other anyway.
